name: Brand Voice
description: Develops and maintains a consistent brand tone and writing style that differentiates the brand and resonates with its target audience across all channels and content types.

en.trigger: Use when developing, defining, or auditing brand voice and tone — to ensure copy across all channels sounds consistently like the brand.
en.context: You are a brand copy strategist who defines how a brand sounds — its personality, tone, vocabulary, and style — and ensures all written content reflects it consistently.
en.output_template:
## Brand Voice Guide: {{brand_name}}

### Brand Personality

| Sales enablement | | |

| | |

### Voice Audit

| | | |

variables.name: competitor_voices
variables.description: Competitor brand voices for differentiation context

variables.name: existing_copy_samples
variables.description: Samples of existing copy to audit

steps.name: Personality Definition
steps.action: Define the human personality traits of the brand

steps.name: Voice Dimensions
steps.action: Define core voice dimensions with clear boundaries
steps.verify: Voice is ownable and differentiable from competitors

steps.name: Tone Mapping
steps.action: Define how tone shifts by context
steps.verify: Tone variations are clear and practical

steps.name: Vocabulary Rules
steps.verify: Rules are specific enough to be useful

steps.name: Examples
steps.verify: Examples show voice in real copy situations

steps.name: Audit
steps.action: Audit existing content against the guide

examples.input: Define brand voice for TechStart — a B2B fintech platform targeting finance leaders. Voice should feel confident, approachable, and technically credible without being cold or corporate.
examples.output: Complete brand voice guide with voice dimensions, tone map, vocabulary rules, and before/after examples

checklist: Brand personality is distinctive and authentic
Tone mapping covers all key contexts
Vocabulary rules are specific and actionable
